extraction.one_line_summary: SAGAN Peter survived all climbs to launch inside attack with right-hand turn approaching, taking stage from reduced sprint group ahead of PINOT and ROGLIČ
tactical_patterns: YATES Adam abandoned while 33 seconds from lead, revealing GC contenders risk overextension defending slim margins on multi-climb stages
SAGAN Peter moved 10th to 3rd before final kilometer right-hand turn, using corner geometry to launch inside attack
QUINTANA Nairo extended 33-second lead despite YATES abandonment, suggesting conservative riding when closest rival eliminates himself
PINOT Thibaut gained 6 seconds via bonuses despite second place, showing time bonuses matter more than placings for chasing GC

rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: Survived multiple climbs to win reduced sprint, positioning from 10th in final kilometer shows exceptional race-reading on hilly finishes
rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: Abandoned from 33 seconds behind GC lead, suggesting mechanical/illness vulnerability rather than pure climbing deficit
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: neutral: Extended lead passively through rival's abandonment while conserving energy in group finish
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: positive: Recovered 6 seconds on QUINTANA through bonuses despite losing stage, demonstrates aggressive bonus-hunting strategy
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: Fourth place after being at sharp end in finale shows climbing form compatible with reduced sprint positioning
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Third place from break/climb selection suggests early-career versatility on mixed terrain stages
